article_title: BioNTech's Personalized mRNA Vaccine Keeps 11 of 14 Triple-Negative Breast Cancer Patients Relapse-Free for Up to Six Years

findings.message: This article was requested by a human editor — apply heightened scrutiny to content accuracy and source quality

content_preview.title: BioNTech's Personalized mRNA Vaccine Keeps 11 of 14 Triple-Negative Breast Cancer Patients Relapse-Free for Up to Six Years
content_preview.summary: A first-in-human trial published in Nature shows BioNTech's individualized neoantigen mRNA vaccine produces durable T cell immunity and sustained disease control in aggressive breast cancer.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A first-in-human clinical trial of BioNTech's personalized mRNA cancer vaccine has demonstrated that individualized neoantigen immunization can produce durable T cell responses and sustained disease control in patients with triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC), one of the most aggressive and difficult-to-treat forms of the disease. The results, [published in Nature](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-025-10004-2), show that 11 of 14 vaccinated patients remained relapse-free for ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Outstanding biomedical article on personalized mRNA cancer vaccine. Exceptional technical depth with clear explanation of immunological mechanisms and individual patient outcomes.
editor_notes.source_verification: 3 sources checked. Nature paper (s41586-025-10004-2): captured in source snapshot — primary research article. Nature News (d41586-026-00477-0): HTTP 303 redirect but captured in source snapshot. PubMed: VERIFIED — confirms title 'Individualized mRNA vaccines evoke durable T cell immunity in adjuvant TNBC', Sahin/Schmidt authors, 14 patients, 11 relapse-free up to 6 years, T cell phenotypes (cytotoxic effector + stem cell memory), three relapse cases with distinct mechanisms, median follow-up. Human-requested with heightened scrutiny.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified against PubMed/Nature: 14 patients, 11/14 relapse-free (10 alive + 1 death unrelated), 62 months median follow-up, up to 20 neoantigens, 8 IV doses over 9 weeks, CD8+ T cells up to 17.5%, three relapse cases with distinct immunological profiles. TNBC 15% of breast cancers confirmed.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and scientifically precise. Appropriately caveats small sample size and lack of control group. No overclaiming.
editor_notes.originality: Cross-references Moderna/Merck melanoma vaccine article. Extends mRNA cancer vaccine coverage to TNBC.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Outstanding human-requested biomedical article. Peer-reviewed Nature paper validates all claims. Approved.
